title: The Pillay Family Trail: Alice's Guildford
blurb: A Looking-Glass adventure for Isaac, Eli, Mum & Dad. Follow the clues to Guildford's secret Alice spots — Isaac cracks the clues, Eli's got a spotting mission at every stop, and there are stickers to collect. Little legs welcome: do as many stops as you fancy.

start.name: Top of the High Street, by the George Abbot statue
start.directions: Buggy-friendly, but the High Street is a cobbled hill — mind the slope with the pram. Nearest car park: Castle (Sydenham Rd). SHORT VERSION for little legs: stops 1–3 are all minutes apart at the top of town; stops 4–5 add a riverside loop. Hold little hands by the river at the last stop.

stops.name: The Robed Statue
stops.clue: Say hello to the big bronze man in the long robe! He's holding something you might read at bedtime. What is it?
stops.answer_hint: It's open in his hands — you read it!
stops.little_ones: Eli — can you WAVE at the big statue man? 👋
stops.grownup: He's George Abbot, born in Guildford in 1562, who became Archbishop of Canterbury — and founded the almshouses right behind you in 1619.
stops.story: George Abbot grew up in Guildford and rose to the very top of the church. This bronze was put up in 1993. The almshouses behind you, which he built in 1619, are still lived in today!

stops.name: The Giant Golden Clock
stops.clue: Look UP! A giant clock pokes right out over the street on a long arm. What colour is it?
stops.little_ones: Eli — point UP at the big shiny clock! ☝️
stops.grownup: A clockmaker called John Aylward gave it to the town in 1683 — and that date is painted on it in real gold leaf.
stops.story: This famous clock has hung over Guildford's High Street since 1683. A clockmaker gave it to the town as a thank-you for letting him work here. It's covered in real gold!

stops.name: The Castle & the Magic Mirror
stops.clue: Up in the castle gardens, find the little girl statue. She's stepping through something you look into to see your own face. What is it?
stops.answer_hint: You see yourself in it — 'Through the Looking-…'
stops.little_ones: Eli — can you find the BIG stone castle tower? 🏰
stops.grownup: The Norman keep dates to the 1130s. The little Alice statue (1990) marks how Lewis Carroll finished 'Through the Looking-Glass' while staying just nearby on Castle Hill.
stops.story: Guildford's castle is nearly 900 years old! Hidden in a quiet corner of the gardens is a statue of Alice stepping through a magic mirror — because the man who wrote Alice, Lewis Carroll, finished one of the books right here in Guildford.

stops.name: The Oldest Building in Town
stops.clue: This little church is the OLDEST building in the whole town — nearly a thousand years old! Is its bumpy old tower made of brick, or stone?
stops.answer_hint: Touch it — it's hard and grey, not red bricks.
stops.little_ones: Eli — can you find the church's big wooden door? 🚪
stops.grownup: The Anglo-Saxon tower dates to about 1040 — roughly 70 years older than the Castle. Lewis Carroll's funeral was held here in 1898.
stops.story: St Mary's tower was built around the year 1040 — that's nearly 1,000 years ago, older than the castle! It's made of rough flint stone. Lewis Carroll's funeral was held here.

stops.name: Follow the White Rabbit!
stops.clue: Down by the river, a bronze Alice is chasing an animal who is late, late, LATE! What animal is it?
stops.answer_hint: 'I'm late! I'm late!' — a white, fluffy…
stops.little_ones: Eli — can you find the bunny rabbit, and hop like him? 🐰
stops.grownup: Edwin Russell's bronze (1984) shows the riverboat afternoon that first sparked the Alice stories — Alice's sister reads beside her.
stops.story: Here by the River Wey, Alice chases the White Rabbit down his hole and into Wonderland. This statue remembers the sunny boat trip where Lewis Carroll first made up the story for a little girl called Alice.

finale.title: You did it, Isaac & Eli! 🎉🐇
finale.message: Five stops, all of Alice's Guildford, explored by the Pillay family. Lewis Carroll would be proud!
finale.reward_idea: An ice cream by the River Wey — explorers' orders! 🍦

_recce_notes: FOOT-VERIFY these GPS pins on the first walk and tighten geofences: Stop 3 Looking-Glass Alice (walled garden, tree cover — coord is the keep, 60 m fence covers grounds), Stop 4 St Mary's (Wikipedia coord 4 dp), Stop 5 White Rabbit (riverside statue not individually geotagged — 70 m fence). Stops 1 & 2 high-confidence.
